title: QUIC Local Interop-Runner Execution-Report Helper Verification
verification_method: Local execution, artifact inspection, and report review.
preconditions: Docker is available on the local machine.
Python 3 is available for the external runner checkout.
`tshark` and `editcap` are available on PATH or in the standard Windows Wireshark install directories for non-DryRun execution.
The local runner checkout exists at `C:\src\quic-interop\quic-interop-runner`.
The supported INT harness slices remain available through the existing local harness path.
scope: Verify that the local helper can build the harness image, invoke the external runner through image replacement, and capture runner JSON, Markdown, stdout/stderr, and log artifacts under a repo-local execution-report tree without modifying the runner repository. This verification also covers split client/server local-role runs and the harness's empty-server-REQUESTS tolerance on supported paths.
procedure: Run the helper against the local runner checkout with the default both-role replacement slot and the supported testcase subset.

Run the helper in client-local mode and server-local mode against the established peer slots, then confirm each run creates a timestamped artifact root under `artifacts/interop-runner/` and that it contains the runner JSON report, the captured Markdown or console table, the log directory tree, and the harness image build output.
Confirm supported server-role harness paths still start when the runner supplies an empty `REQUESTS` list, while client-role paths continue to require request URLs.

Inspect the preserved server-local handshake rerun at `artifacts/interop-runner/20260422-110409619-server-nginx/` to confirm the helper still captures a complete report bundle when the runner passes `REQUESTS_SERVER=""`, the managed harness reaches its listening state, and any later protocol failure is recorded inside the preserved artifacts rather than misclassified as a startup or reporting failure.

Inspect the captured output to verify unsupported testcases still map to the runner's honest unsupported result, build failures remain build failures rather than pass results, and any advisory-only `FileNotFoundError` downgrade stays tied to testcase-specific preserved evidence.

Inspect the preserved server-local helper reruns at `artifacts/interop-runner/20260422-135904895-server-nginx/`, `artifacts/interop-runner/20260422-140055122-server-nginx/`, and `artifacts/interop-runner/20260422-141632722-server-nginx/` to confirm the helper now exits successfully with advisories for server-role handshake, retry, and transfer once the preserved output proves the managed lane completed and only the upstream runner's post-check still failed.

For the client-role `multiconnect` advisory path, confirm the helper itself exits `0`, prints an advisory that cites the preserved `output.txt`, and leaves the upstream runner's own inner exit code and failed report bundle intact for audit.
Confirm missing Docker, Python, `tshark`, or `editcap` prerequisites fail during helper preflight before the Docker image build or runner launch starts.
If the tree is temporarily mid-edit and the first run fails for an unrelated build reason, wait briefly and rerun once to confirm the helper still behaves honestly after the transient issue clears.
expected_result: The helper produces repo-local execution-report artifacts for local runner runs without requiring any change to the runner repository, supported server-role paths tolerate empty runner REQUESTS, split-role client/server local executions work, missing local prerequisites fail before expensive build or runner work starts, unsupported or build-blocked cases are not misreported as successful runs, and the runner's post-check `FileNotFoundError` is downgraded to advisory only on testcase-specific, evidence-backed paths.
status_summary: Passed on 2026-04-22; the local helper built the harness image, invoked the external runner through image replacement, and captured repo-local execution-report bundles while preserving the runner's noncompliant result honestly. The helper now covers both same-slot and split-role local runs, the harness tolerates empty server-side `REQUESTS` on supported dispatch paths, and the proof includes the narrow advisory-only `FileNotFoundError` classification used by handshake, retry, server-role transfer, client-role transfer, and client-role multiconnect when preserved output proves completion. The fresh client-role quic-go multiconnect evidence at `artifacts/interop-runner/20260422-100630446-client-chrome/` shows the helper exiting successfully while the preserved upstream runner report remains failed because its own post-check threw `FileNotFoundError` after all managed downloads completed. The preserved server-role quic-go handshake rerun at `artifacts/interop-runner/20260422-110409619-server-nginx/` narrowed the last startup/helper uncertainty by proving the harness reaches `listening on 0.0.0.0:443` with `REQUESTS_SERVER=""`, and the later server-role helper reruns at `artifacts/interop-runner/20260422-135904895-server-nginx/`, `artifacts/interop-runner/20260422-140055122-server-nginx/`, and `artifacts/interop-runner/20260422-141632722-server-nginx/` now show handshake, retry, and transfer all completing on the managed path while only the upstream runner's post-check still fails.

supplemental_sections.heading: Deferred Coverage
supplemental_sections.content: No runner repository registry change, no new testcase enablement, no qlog-format changes, and no transport/runtime behavior changes are in scope for this verification slice.
